content_markdown:
The diagram shows the pressure / volume ($p/V$) diagram for one cycle PQRS of an engine.

In which sections of the cycle is work done **on** the engine?

diagram_metadata.description: A p-V diagram showing a thermodynamic cycle with four points: P, Q, R, and S. The cycle moves from P to Q (compression), Q to R (isobaric expansion), R to S (expansion), and S to P (isochoric cooling).

vibe_explanation: Work is done on a gas (the engine's working fluid) when its volume decreases ($W = -\int p dV$). Looking at the $p/V$ diagram, the volume decreases only during the transition from P to Q. In the section S to P, the volume is constant, so no work is done. In sections Q to R and R to S, the volume increases, meaning work is done by the engine.
